summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn welcomed the Impact Church congregation on Christmas Eve, celebrating recent baptisms and reflecting on the church's journey. He opened with the core identity of Impact Church, rooted in Philippians 2:15, which calls believers to 'shine like stars in a dark universe.' He contrasted the cross—the symbol of what Jesus did for us—with the star, which represents what we are called to do for Him: to be the light of the world. Pastor Travis observed that the world is growing darker with emotional, relational, and spiritual struggles, but Jesus, the Light of the World, offers hope. He shared a personal testimony of his mother's resilience through profound hardship, illustrating how a life surrendered to Christ becomes a bright light. The message concluded with a call to move from being merely surrounded by the light to having the light of Jesus within through surrender and repentance, inviting many to make a first-time commitment to Christ.
mainPoints: Jesus calls believers to be the light of the world, actively shining for Him rather than just receiving what He has done.
Jesus is the light that overcomes emotional darkness (like fear and anxiety), relational darkness (like betrayal and loss), and spiritual darkness (sin and separation from God).
True discipleship requires denying oneself, taking up one's cross, and following Jesus, which moves a person from being surrounded by light to being surrendered to it.

versesMentioned.text: so that you may become blameless and pure, 'children of God without fault in a warped and crooked generation.' Then you will shine among them like stars in the sky

You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.'

In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. He was with God in the beginning. Through him all things were made; without him nothing was made that has been made. In him was life, and that life was the light of all mankind. The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.

But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days. People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy, without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good, treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God— having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people. They are the kind who worm their way into homes and gain control over gullible women, who are loaded down with sins and are swayed by all kinds of evil desires, always learning but never able to come to a knowledge of the truth.
For you were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord. Live as children of light
The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of deep darkness a light has dawned.
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.
For to us a child is born, to us a son is given, and the government will be on his shoulders. And he will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.
The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.

Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
The lamp of a wicked man is snuffed out; the flame of his fire stops burning. The light in his tent becomes dark; the lamp beside him goes out.
Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.
Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the Lord rises upon you.
The Lord is my light and my salvation— whom shall I fear? The Lord is the stronghold of my life— of whom shall I be afraid?

Monday.scripture: Matthew 5:14-16 - 'You are the light of the world... let your light shine before others...'
Monday.focus: Understanding your identity as a light.
Monday.action: Identify one specific way you can intentionally 'shine' for Christ in your workplace or neighborhood today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to see myself as Your light. Give me courage and opportunity to shine for You today.

Tuesday.scripture: John 8:12 - 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness...'
Tuesday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet reflection. Identify one area of 'darkness' (worry, a sinful habit, a strained relationship) and consciously choose to bring it to Jesus.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, You are my light. I choose to follow You out of the darkness of [name the area]. Lead me today.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 34:18 - 'The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.'
Wednesday.focus: God's nearness in emotional and relational pain.
Wednesday.action: Reach out to one person you know who is hurting—send a text, make a call, or write a note of encouragement.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank You for being close to the brokenhearted. Use me to extend Your comfort and love to someone who needs it.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4-7 - 'Love is patient, love is kind...'
Thursday.focus: Letting God's love shine through your relationships.
Thursday.action: Practice one attribute of love from 1 Corinthians 13 (e.g., patience, not keeping a record of wrongs) in a challenging relationship.
Thursday.prayer: God, You are love. Fill me with Your love so I can reflect it, especially where it's difficult. Help me to love like You do.

Friday.scripture: Revelation 2:5 - 'Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first...'
Friday.action: Take an honest look at your spiritual passion. Is your 'light' dimming? If so, tell God about it and make one practical step to re-engage (e.g., open your Bible, pray for 5 minutes).
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, search my heart. Show me where I need to repent and return to my first love for You. Rekindle my passion for Christ.

Saturday.scripture: Isaiah 60:1 - 'Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the Lord rises upon you.'
Saturday.focus: Stepping into your calling to shine.
Saturday.action: Prepare for tomorrow (Sunday) by deciding to be fully present in worship, ready to receive from God and be a light to others.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I arise today in Your strength. Let Your glory rise upon me. Prepare my heart to worship You and to shine for You this weekend.
